content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to create a zoo simulation using Multilevel Inheritance. There are various types of animals, each with common and specific behaviors.


Create a base class `Animal` with a method `void speak()` that prints: "Animal speaks".
Create a class `Dog` that inherits from `Animal` and overrides `speak()` to print: "Dog barks".
Create another class `Puppy` that inherits from `Dog` and overrides `speak()` to print: "Puppy yaps".
You are given the type of animal as input in lowercase ("animal", "dog", or "puppy"). Your task is implement multilevel inheritance and create an object of that type and call its `speak()` method.


Input Format
A single line containing a string: the type of animal.



Output Format
Output the result of calling the `speak()` method.
constraints: Each string is exactly one of: "animal", "dog", "puppy"
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single word in lowercase: "animal", "dog" or "puppy".
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output on a separate line the sound produced by the corresponding animal:
- "Animal speaks" for "animal"
- "Dog barks" for "dog"
- "Puppy yaps" for "puppy"

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with "puppy" creates a Puppy object; its speak() prints "Puppy yaps".
One test case with "dog" creates a Dog object; its speak() prints "Dog barks".
One test case with "animal" creates an Animal object; its speak() prints "Animal speaks".

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Puppy yaps
Dog barks
Animal speaks
Puppy yaps
Dog barks

hidden.label: mixed order
hidden.expectedOutput:
Dog barks
Animal speaks
Puppy yaps
Animal speaks

hidden.label: all same type
hidden.expectedOutput:
Puppy yaps
Puppy yaps
Puppy yaps

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the animal type. Using multilevel inheritance, create an object of the appropriate class (Animal, Dog or Puppy) and call its virtual speak() method. The overridden method prints the required string. The solution works in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few pointers/objects are created, no additional data structures.
